findings.why_it_matters: Large high-entropy zones can represent encryption, packing, or random overwrite behavior.
Timestamp or metadata mismatches can indicate timestomping, cloning artifacts, or anti-forensic manipulation.
Structured overwrite patterns can indicate intentional destruction or sanitization of evidence.
Deletion bursts can be normal cleanup or deliberate post-incident anti-forensic behavior.
Suspicious low-entropy slack regions may indicate hidden payloads or tampering in slack space.
A few high-entropy regions may be normal (e.g., compressed files).
